channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: The World's First Electric Articulated Haulers Just Went Into Serial Production. The Team That Built Them Got a Little Emotional.
item.dc:creator: Volvo Construction Equipment
item.description: Volvo Construction Equipment has started serial production of the A30 and A40 Electric, the world's first battery electric articulated haulers in their size class. The machines deliver the same performance as their diesel counterparts. The team worked flat, pulling desks right next to the assembly line during development. The moment the first machine was assembled, flashed with new software, and started with a key turn is something the engineers say they will never forget. Both models are now being readied for delivery to the first customers.

item.title: Is the Ioniq 6N a Supercar? Autotrader Took It to a Racetrack to Find Out.
item.dc:creator: Autotrader
item.description: Autotrader's Rory takes the £65,000 Hyundai Ioniq 6N to the Castellolí circuit near Barcelona: 650 PS, 0-to-62 in 3.2 seconds, drift optimizer, N Grin Boost, a simulated 8-speed DCT that beeps when you need to shift, and 260 kW rapid charging for 10-to-80% in around 18 minutes. He also sits down with engineer Tyrone Johnson, the man behind the Focus RS and Mustang, to hear why the fake gears and synthetic sound are serious engineering and not a gimmick. Verdict: not quite a supercar. Definitely a super car.

item.title: BYD Is Selling Out Across Europe. Americans Have No Idea What They're Missing.
item.dc:creator: CBS Sunday Morning
item.description: CBS Sunday Morning visits a BYD dealership in London where a Lexus owner is trading up to a Sealion 7. BYD, which started as a battery company and owns its whole supply chain, can reportedly build EVs for around 25% less than Western competitors. In the US, 100% tariffs effectively close the market to Chinese imports. In Norway, 97% of new car sales are electric. In China, roughly half. The US is under 10%. One British BYD owner on his American family's reaction: jealous.

item.title: This E-Bike Hits 67 mph and Claims Hellcat-Level Torque. Logan Taylor Rode It in Overalls.
item.dc:creator: Logan Taylor
item.description: Logan Taylor unboxes and rides the Y-Volt Surge V, which Y-Volt bills as the most powerful e-bike in its size class at 48 horsepower. GPS top speed: 67 mph. Y-Volt claims 700 ft-lb of torque. The package includes oversized four-piston brakes, RFID and physical key unlock, forged carbon components, a dark-mode screen, and lit side panels. Sport mode has a noticeable mid-throttle power surge. Sponsored by Y-Volt, but Logan had full editorial control.

item.title: Rivian Is Building a 40,000 Sq Ft Showroom, Service Hub, and Charging Stop Right Off I-4 in Orlando
item.dc:creator: RivianTrackr
item.description: Jose Castillo at RivianTrackr stopped by the cleared lot off I-4 across from Millenia Mall where Rivian is constructing a 40,000 sq ft facility combining a showroom, service center, and charging hub. Construction goes vertical later this year. It is Rivian's third Orlando-area location, after Jetstream Drive and the Shader Road service center that opened last July. Rivian's name comes from the Indian River Lagoon in Florida, making Central Florida a little more than just another expansion dot.

channel.title: No Generator, No Problem: Five EV Campers Tell It Like It Is
channel.dc:creator: Out of Spec BITS
channel.description: Tom from Out of Spec hosts four fellow EV campers: Allan, who pulled a 32-foot, 8,000 lb loaded trailer with his Lightning Flash across 4,000 towing miles last year; Eric, who gets around 180 miles per charge towing a 25-foot Airstream behind a 2025 Rivian R1T tri-motor; Austin, who sleeps in a Model Y on a custom plywood platform with a $500 bolt-on tent; and Skye, who built a two-bunk eSprinter van with 8 kWh of EcoFlow power. Tom powers his whole camper off the Lightning's Pro Power on board and burned only 40% battery over six days at Daytona. Group consensus: aerodynamics matter more than trailer weight, and charging stops make better lunches than gas stations do.

item.title: 379,000 Miles on the Original Battery. Tesla's Health Test Had an Answer.
item.dc:creator: Drive Protected
item.description: Drive Protected runs Tesla's built-in battery health test on a Model 3 with 610,433 km on the clock. The test drains the pack to zero, charges it to 100%, and reports usable capacity against new. Getting started required breaking into the car by hand after the 12V battery died, snapping a tow hook cover in the process. Tesla service cleared a coolant fault before the test could run. Next morning: 70% battery health remaining. The screen shows 89% as the new ceiling for this pack. According to Tesla, it is still the original battery.

item.title: The Man Who Invented the Modern Solar Panel Is 77 and Lives Near a Beach
item.dc:creator: ABC News In-depth
item.description: ABC News profiles Martin Green, the University of New South Wales researcher whose PERC cell design became the foundation of modern solar manufacturing. His lab held the world record for silicon solar cell efficiency for 31 years, from 1983 to 2014. One of his Chinese PhD students took the knowledge home and became the first solar billionaire. Green published instead of patenting, which is why the solar industry exists at its current scale and why almost nobody has heard of his name. In 2024, about 600 gigawatts of solar capacity were added worldwide, with some single days matching what the entire world installed in a year back in 2004.

item.title: China's Wind and Solar Beat Coal. Now Canada Has to Decide What to Do About That.
item.dc:creator: CBC News: The National
item.description: In 2025, China's combined wind and solar capacity surpassed coal for the first time. Envision, the world's second-largest wind turbine maker, runs an AI-managed wind and green hydrogen project in the Gobi Desert. Company founder Le Chang has discussed bringing that model to Canada's remote wilderness with Prime Minister Mark Carney, who said Canada plans to double its energy grid over 15 years. The concern: Ontario Premier Doug Ford calls it giving a geopolitical rival an operating system for Canada's power supply. Meanwhile, China is still approving new coal plants.

item.title: Delhi Has a Plan B for Electrification. It Doesn't Involve Buying New Trucks.
item.dc:creator: DW REV - Cars & Mobility
item.description: More than 15 million vehicles crowd Delhi's streets and fewer than 8% are electric, well short of the city's 30% target for 2030. IX Energy strips out diesel powertrains, installs electric ones, and extends a truck's working life by up to seven years. Operating costs drop from around 12 rupees per kilometer to about 4. A conversion takes roughly two weeks and costs about 40% less than buying a new electric truck. The idea is not scaling fast: certified retrofitters are scarce, regulations are vague, and public charging is limited.

item.title: A 3,170 kg Electric Truck vs a Corvette Z06 in the Quarter Mile
item.dc:creator: carwow
item.description: carwow lines up the Rivian R1T quad motor against the Chevrolet Corvette Z06 over the standing quarter mile. The Rivian weighs 3,170 kg, produces 1,025 horsepower and 1,624 Nm of torque. The Z06 packs a 5.5-litre naturally aspirated V8 with 670 horsepower and a launch control system adjustable from 3,500 to 5,000 rpm with variable wheel slip, driven by NASCAR racer Jesse Iwuji, who owns the car. Standing quarter mile: Rivian 10.6 seconds, Corvette 11.3. Jesse's post-race discovery: turning stability control fully off changed everything. Way too late.

item.title: Honda's Tiny EV Has Fake Gears, a Boost Button, and the Cheek to Call Itself Super
item.dc:creator: What Car?
item.description: What Car? gets an early look at the Honda Super N before UK showrooms open in July. Built on the bones of the Japanese N-One E city car, the Super N runs wider, sits lower, and promises something the N-One E very much does not: genuine driving fun. A boost button lifts the single motor from 63 to 94 horsepower. Paddles simulate a seven-speed sequential gearbox, and an active sound system recreates an engine note to match. Ambient lighting turns from blue to purple in boost mode. Honda quotes a combined range of 128 miles and says pricing will come in under £20,000, undercutting the cheapest Fiat 500e. In showrooms July 2026.

item.title: Stop. Before You Buy a New EV, Watch This.
item.dc:creator: ReDriven
item.description: Redriven makes the case that the Australian used EV market is the best-kept secret going. BMW iX models that were $150,000 new are listing around $50,000. A Mercedes-Benz EQS 53 AMG that retailed near $400,000 in 2022 can be found used for a little over $90,000. The Hyundai Ioniq 5 has dropped from around $70,000 new to $35,000. Top picks: Genesis GV60 for 800V charging at half price, GV70 EV for stronger residuals, and used Tesla Model 3s from under $30,000. Check accident history carefully, confirm all recalls are done, and stick to factory-backed brands.

item.title: Inside the Tesla Semi Factory Nobody Had Seen Until Now
item.dc:creator: Core Memory
item.description: An exclusive walkthrough of Tesla's Semi factory at Gigafactory Nevada in Sparks, where mass production is now ramping up. The tour covers the overhead conveyance system, where frames grow into 10,000-plus-pound assemblies as components are added at operator height, battery marriage where three parallel packs using Cybertruck cells are bolted into the frame, and the light tunnel at the end of general assembly. Standard range: 325 miles. Long range: 500 miles. A Tesla CDL driver describes the experience: effortless torque, remarkably quiet, and regen braking that removes steep-grade anxiety entirely.

item.title: Hyundai's New Concept Is Named After a Planet and Arrives in Gold. The Production Version Comes to Beijing.
item.dc:creator: OTOFOOTAGE
item.description: Hyundai debuted the Ioniq Venus Concept in China, unveiling a new design direction called Art of Steel that replaces the parametric pixel language used across current Ioniq models. The exterior uses a single-curve silhouette, precision-cut edges, a lightweight frame roof, and a transparent rear spoiler, all in radiant gold paint. Inside, a dual-curved panoramic display pairs with a 3D augmented reality head-up display projecting navigation onto the windshield. Hyundai says the Venus supports both a full BEV powertrain and an extended range electric setup. A production-ready version is expected at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show.

item.title: You Already Know More About EVs Than You Think. Here's a Translation Guide.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Hunka
item.description: EV jargon sounds intimidating until you realize it is just the same car stuff with different names. kWh is your fuel tank. kW is how fast the pump flows. Phantom drain is your porch light. Here is the plain-English translation guide nobody wrote for you.

item.title: This Subaru Station Wagon Is Faster Than Any STI They Ever Made. It's Also Kind of a Mess.
item.dc:creator: Gjeebs
item.description: Gjeebs drives the 2026 Subaru Trailseeker from Phoenix to Sedona and back with a charging stop to build a complete picture. The good stuff is genuinely good: 375 horsepower, Subaru's symmetrical AWD, 0-60 in 4.4 seconds, exceptional ride quality, and a 10-to-80% charging record of 25 minutes and 7 seconds — four minutes faster than the 2026 Model Y. The bad stuff is just as real: no route planning whatsoever, an infotainment system that earns every bad word thrown at it, and that record charge adds only 161 miles of range. Real-world, the Trailseeker ran 240 miles from 100% to empty on the Sedona loop, mostly without AC. Priced from $39,995 to $45,000, it's the only EV station wagon on the US market and falls just short of being something special.

item.title: Cupra's New Raval Is the Sporty Evil Twin the ID.Polo Always Needed
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Autogefühl walks through the all-new Cupra Raval: 4 meters long, 15mm lower than the ID.Polo, 10mm wider in the track. Top VZ trim runs 226 hp, adaptive DCC suspension, a front diff lock, 0-to-62 mph in under 7 seconds, and a 110 mph top speed. The 56 kWh NMC battery charges at 130 kW for a 10-to-80% time of 23 minutes. Trunk fits a cabin trolley. Flush door handles appear here alone among its platform siblings. Pricing starts around €26,000 for the small battery; the fully loaded VZ is expected near €38,000.

item.title: Two Journalists Slept in a Hyundai Ioniq 9 and an Inster in February. One of Them Got Eight Hours.
item.dc:creator: Motorpoint
item.description: Motorpoint tests chabak, the Korean car-camping trend, using a Hyundai Ioniq 9 and a Hyundai Inster in a cold, wet car park in February. The Ioniq 9 brings a 111 kWh battery, 360 miles of range, vehicle-to-load capability, and electrically folding rear seats for a flat sleeping floor. The Inster brings a 49 kWh battery, 223 miles of official range, and flat front and rear seats. Both use utility mode to heat the cabin overnight. Verdict: warmer than expected, quieter than a tent, and you still need all your camping gear.

item.title: 0 to 97% in 9 Minutes. Denza's Flash Charger Just Made Its European Debut Off the Grid.
item.dc:creator: electricfelix
item.description: electricfelix gets a front-row seat at the first Denza flash charging system outside China. The setup is fully off-grid, drawing from two 190 kWh BYD Blade Battery Gen 2 storage units and a 2 MW power converter. Cables never touch the ground. Live results: 50% in 3 minutes 20 seconds, 80% in 6.5 minutes, 97% in 9 minutes 22 seconds. The car's active cooling shut off before 97% because the battery was unbothered. Currently being installed at Denza dealerships.

item.title: Tesla Is Working on a Smaller, Cheaper SUV — and the Timing Says Everything
item.dc:creator: nexusEVnews
item.description: Reuters reports Tesla is developing an all-new compact SUV smaller and cheaper than the Model Y — a notable reversal after scrapping its $25,000 EV project in 2024. The vehicle would be 4.28 meters long, single motor, lighter than the Model Y, and built in China. Production is unlikely this year. We look at what this means for Tesla's sliding deliveries, how it stacks up against BYD and VW in a segment where both have a serious head start, and what analysts will be watching to separate real commitment from another false start.
